sglang.title: [Misc] use parallel build for cmake in sgl-kernel
sglang.author: FlamingoPg
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Now we already build sgl-kernel with Ninja in parallel. When user not install ninja, sgl-kernel makefile instruct make build will not let user build in parallel.
Reference: https://scikit-build-core.readthedocs.io/en/latest/guide/faqs.html#multithreaded-builds
Just add CMAKE_BUILD_PARALL

vllm.title: [TPU][V1][CI] Update regression test baseline for v6 CI

vllm.title: [Core] Prevent side-channel attacks via cache salting
vllm.author: dr75
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Context
Prefix caching in vLLM improves inference performance by reusing KV blocks across requests. However, this reuse introduces a potential privacy risk in shared environments, where an attacker could infer prompt reuse via timing side channels as demonstrated in Leaking Secrets from Prefix Cache

vllm.title: [V1][Metrics] add support for kv event publishing
vllm.author: alec-flowers
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
RFC: KVBlocks and Metrics Publishing In Inference Frameworks

Added KVCacheEvent, BlockStored, BlockRemoved, and AllBlocksCleared msgspec classes
Created a queue in the BlockPool and write these events in the appropriate functions
Bubble the events up to the scheduler where they are appended to Engi

vllm.title: [Feature] The Qwen3 reasoning parser supports  guided decoding
vllm.author: chaunceyjiang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIx  #17369 (comment)

is_reasoning_end is used by guided decoding backend to check reasoning stage. This Qwen3ReasoningParser don't implement this method.

vllm.title: [Docs] Add command for running mypy tests from CI
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Whenever one of the mypy jobs that only runs in CI fails, I'm doing
history | grep mypy to remember what command I used to run the mypy
tests from CI. This adds the command to the docs to help others (and
myself) find it when needed.
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

to format with %s instead of f-string
Signed-off-by: Aaron Pham contact@aarnphm.xyz

vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
commit bd4d899
Author: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com
Date:   Tue Apr 22 15:44:10 2025 -0400
[V1] Allow turning off pickle fallback in vllm.v1.serial_utils

`pickle` is a well-known insecure method of serialization if there is
any chance we may be deserializing untrusted data. It is the cause of

vllm.title: [v1][Spec Decode] Make sliding window compatible with eagle prefix caching
vllm.author: heheda12345
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
#17137 drops the last matched block to support eagle. This strategy is not correct for sliding window layers.
When sliding window size is 4 and block_size is 2, we need at least 2 blocks. However,
find_longest_cache_hit of sliding_window_layers will return [NULL_BLOCK, NULL_BLOCK, BLOCK_A, BLOCK_B],

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Reorganize models tests
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Instead of nesting by <arch/task> -> <modality>, we now nest the directories by <modality> -> <task> to better align with our test grouping in CI.
Combine audio and vision test directories together since some models support both.
Don't separate by architecture anymore since V1 should have built-in e

vllm.title: [Build] Require setuptools >= 77.0.3 for PEP 639
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
In PR #17259, the license specification in pyproject.toml was updated
to use the new format specified by PEP 639. The previous format has been
deprecated. Support for this new format requires setuptools>=77.0.3.
Update the requirements accordingly.
For more details on the format, see:
https://packag

vllm.title: [ROCm] Effort to reduce the number of environment variables in command line
vllm.author: hongxiayang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is to set two environment variables in the Docker file so that users can reduce the number of environment variables when running scripts.
ENV that can improve safe tensor loading, and end-to-end time
ENV SAFETENSORS_FAST_GPU=1

ENV that needed for multi-process on cuda-like platform
ENV VLLM_WO

vllm.title: [BugFix] fix speculative decoding memory leak when speculation is disabled
vllm.author: noah-yoshida
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
There is a memory leak associated with self.previous_hidden_states on the spec_decode_worker when using --speculative-disable-by-batch-size.
It comes from the fact that we do not clean up the tensors except inside of the speculative decoding codepath, but when it is disabled via batch size, we go th
